title: Upon Tooly.
content:
The eggs of pheasants wry-nosed Tooly sells,
    But ne'er so much as licks the speckled shells:
    Only, if one prove addled, that he eats
    With superstition, as the cream of meats.
    The cock and hen he feeds; but not a bone
    He ever picked, as yet, of anyone.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon Tooly.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
